source.title: Increasing muscle activity correlations during spontaneous movements in the first six months of life

confidence_rationale: EMG study of n=40 full-term infants from 1 week to 6 months; longitudinal within-subject design with 12 muscles measured across ipsilateral/contralateral limb pairs. Directly tracks the transition from uncorrelated to coordinated spontaneous limb activity. Source quality moderate (Neuroscience Letters, not top-tier); mapping fidelity good because the progressive emergence of inter-limb coordination is the mechanism underlying the random-to-structured transition REE needs to understand.
failure_signatures: Study is purely observational EMG; no intervention, so causal direction of the correlation increase (maturation vs experience-driven) is unresolved.
Spontaneous vs voluntary distinction is operational only (spontaneous = no salient external trigger), not mechanistically verified.

mapping.source_claim_statement: From 1 week to 6 months, ipsilateral and contralateral limb muscle activity correlations increase progressively in both flexors and extensors, consistent with maturation of physiologically relevant neuromuscular network connections underlying the transition from spontaneous-like to voluntary goal-directed movements.
mapping.ree_translation: The random-to-structured transition is not a discrete switch but a continuous coordination gradient. For REE, this implies that the infant-stage substrate should track inter-module coordination as a metric of developmental progress, not just individual action entropy. The emergence of cross-limb correlations is the structural scaffold that the option store is built on.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The REE architecture has no direct analog of limb-coupling; the closest mapping is cross-module prediction error correlation or latent state coherence. Transfer from neuromuscular coordination to latent space coordination is indirect.
mapping.source_context: Russian cohort, full-term healthy infants; surface EMG with passive leg movement paradigm to test cross-limb coupling.

confidence_components.notes: Solid within-domain EMG study; the mapping from cross-limb muscle correlation to REE module coordination requires a conceptual bridge step.
